page: Microsoft Azure F32ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Fadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This configuration features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3780 GB of local disk capacity and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. It operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3541.18, placing it in the top 10% of tested instances. Multi-core performance is also strong, scoring 111314.11 in stress-ng multi-core tests, which ranks in the top 25%. The memory subsystem shows robust capabilities, with a cached small-block read bandwidth of 6116.97 GB/sec and large-block read and write bandwidths of 214.06 GB/sec and 125.71 GB/sec respectively, both falling within the top 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform in the top 10%, with gzip compression reaching 38.0 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression achieving 16.54 GB/sec.

The F32ads_v7 offers a balanced resource profile with high-frequency dedicated cores and substantial local storage, making it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring consistent CPU performance without hyperthreading overhead. The inclusion of 3780 GB of local storage provides qualitative cost advantages for data-intensive applications that would otherwise require external storage attachments. This server is suited for workloads such as relational databases, multi-threaded data processing, compression tasks, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Microsoft Azure F32ads_v7 is an x86_64 server instance from the Fadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng score of 3541.18, and strong multi-core performance at 111314.11. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 214.06 GB/sec for large-block reads. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 10% of tested servers. This configuration is suited for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring high-frequency dedicated compute resources and substantial local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32ads_v7 is a dedicated 32-vCPU server featuring a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.

tagline: A dedicated thirty-two core AMD EPYC server featuring high-frequency compute, robust memory bandwidth, and substantial local storage capacity.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core.
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core benchmark results.
Suited for databases, data compression, and multi-threaded processing.
